title: grevillea exul subsp. rubiginosa
intro: Grevillea exul subsp. rubiginosa, also known as the red-flowered Grevillea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Grevillea exul subsp. rubiginosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties which may help to prevent infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas or infusions, and it is also used in topical applications for skin irritations and wounds.
